segments.text: Then the word of the LORD came to me, saying,
Now you, son of man, take up a lament for Tyre.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Tell Tyre, who dwells at the gateway to the sea, merchant of the peoples on many coasts, that this is what the Lord GOD says:
You have said, O Tyre,‘I am perfect in beauty.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders perfected your beauty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They constructed all your plankingwith cypress[^66] from Senir.[^67] They took a cedar from Lebanonto make a mast for you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Of oaks from Bashanthey made your oars; of wood from the coasts of Cyprus[^68] they made your deck, inlaid with ivory.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Of embroidered fine linen from Egyptthey made your sail,
which served as your banner. Of blue and purple from the coasts of Elishahthey made your awning.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The men of Sidon and Arvadwere your oarsmen. Your men of skill, O Tyre, were there as your captains.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The elders of Gebal were aboard as shipwrights, repairing your leaks. All the ships of the sea and their sailorscame alongside to barter for your merchandise.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Men of Persia, Lydia, and Putserved as warriors in your army. They hung their shields and helmets on your walls; they gave you splendor.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Men of Arvad and Helechmanned your walls all around, and the men of Gammadwere in your towers. They hung their shields around your walls; they perfected your beauty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Tarshish was your merchant because of your great wealth of goods; they exchanged silver, iron, tin, and lead for your wares.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Javan,[^69] Tubal, and Meshech were your merchants. They exchanged slaves and bronze utensils for your merchandise.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The men of Beth-togarmah exchanged horses, war horses, and mules for your wares.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The men of Dedan[^70] were your clients; many coastlands were your market; they paid you with ivory tusks and ebony.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Aram[^71] was your customer because of your many products; they exchanged turquoise, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and rubies for your wares.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged wheat from Minnith, cakes and honey, oil and balm for your merchandise.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Because of your many products and your great wealth of goods, Damascus traded with you wine from Helbon, wool from Zahar,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and casks of wine from Izal for your wares.[^72] Wrought iron, cassia, and sweet cane[^73] were exchanged for your merchandise.[^74]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Dedan was your merchant in saddlecloths for riding.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your customers, trading in lambs, rams, and goats.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; for your wares they exchanged gold, the finest of all spices, and precious stones.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Haran, Canneh, and Eden traded with you, and so did the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In your marketplace they traded with you fine garments of blue, embroidered work, and multicolored rugs with cords tightly twisted and knotted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The ships of Tarshish[^75] carried your merchandise. And you were filled with heavy cargoin the heart of the sea.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Your oarsmen have brought youonto the high seas, but the east wind will shatter youin the heart of the sea.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Your wealth, wares, and merchandise, your sailors, captains, and shipwrights, your merchants and all the warriors within you, with all the other people on board, will sink into the heart of the seaon the day of your downfall.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The countryside will shakewhen your sailors cry out.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All who handle the oarswill abandon their ships. The sailors and all the captains of the seawill st and on the shore.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will raise their voices for you and cry out bitterly. They will throw dust on their heads and roll in ashes.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will shave their heads for you and wrap themselves in sackcloth. They will weep over youwith anguish of soul and bitter mourning.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
As they wail and mourn over you, they will take up a lament for you:
‘Who was ever like Tyre, silenced in the middle of the sea?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When your wares went out to sea, you satisfied many nations. You enriched the kings of the earthwith your abundant wealth and merchandise.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now you are shattered by the seasin the depths of the waters; your merchandise and the people among youhave gone down with you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: All the people of the coastlandsare appalled over you. Their kings shudder with fear; their faces are contorted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Those who trade among the nationshiss at you; you have come to a horrible end and will be no more.’
segments.speaker: Unknown
